No Poverty.reason: No direct or strongly inferred evidence in the course description or objectives links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or strongly inferred evidence in the course description or objectives links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course's focus on 'moral psychology' and 'transformation' offers a weak, inferred link to promoting mental health and well-being (Target 3.4).

Quality Education.reason: As a university seminar, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops critical thinking and analytical skills for sustainable development (Target 4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course content or structure addresses gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence found in the course description or objectives related to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence found in the course description or objectives related to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: No direct or strongly inferred evidence links the course to economic growth or decent work.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No evidence found in the course description or objectives related to industry, innovation, or infrastructure.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The philosophical study of 'politics,' 'ethics,' and 'institutional order' provides foundational understanding relevant to social inclusion and reducing inequalities (Targets 10.2, 10.3).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No evidence found in the course description or objectives related to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No evidence found in the course description or objectives related to consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No evidence found in the course description or objectives related to climate change.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence found in the course description or objectives related to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence found in the course description or objectives related to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course directly addresses 'politics,' 'governance,' 'institutional order,' and 'statecraft,' aligning with Target 16.6 on effective, accountable, and transparent institutions.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The seminar's explicit collaboration between NCCU and NYCU exemplifies inter-institutional partnership and knowledge sharing (Targets 17.6, 17.17).
